}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_plan.go",
  "newString": "NodeResourceInstanceBase",
  "oldString": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// nodeExpandPlannableResource represents an addrs.ConfigResource and implements\n// DynamicExpand to a subgraph containing all of the addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n// resulting from both the containing module and resource-specific expansion.\ntype nodeExpandPlannableResource struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResource\n\n\t// ForceCreateBeforeDestroy might be set via our GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\n\t// during graph construction, if dependencies require us to force this\n\t// on regardless of what the configuration says.\n\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy *bool\n\n\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n\tskipRefresh bool\n\n\tpreDestroyRefresh bool\n\n\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n\t// for any instances.\n\tskipPlanChanges bool\n\n\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\n\t// We attach dependencies to the Resource during refresh, since the\n\t// instances are instantiated during DynamicExpand.\n\t// FIXME: These would be better off converted to a generic Set data\n\t// structure in the future, as we need to compare for equality and take the\n\t// union of multiple groups of dependencies.\n\tdependencies []addrs.ConfigResource\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyerCBD         = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable    = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferenceable        = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeImportReferencer     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies   = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeTargetable           = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n\t_ graphNodeExpandsInstances     = (*nodeExpandPlannableResource)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) Name() string {\n\treturn n.NodeAbstractResource.Name() + \" (expand)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandsInstances() {\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.dependencies = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) CreateBeforeDestroy() bool {\n\tif n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy != nil {\n\t\treturn *n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\n\t}\n\n\t// If we have no config, we just assume no\n\tif n.Config == nil || n.Config.Managed == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDestroyerCBD\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) ModifyCreateBeforeDestroy(v bool) error {\n\tn.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy = &v\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// First, make sure the count and the foreach don't refer to the same\n\t// resource. The config maybe nil if we are generating configuration, or\n\t// deleting a resource.\n\tif n.Config != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.Count))\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateMetaSelfRef(n.Addr.Resource, n.Config.ForEach))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Expand the current module.\n\texpander := ctx.InstanceExpander()\n\tmoduleInstances := expander.ExpandModule(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\n\t// The possibility of partial-expanded modules and resources is guarded by a\n\t// top-level option for the whole plan, so that we can preserve mainline\n\t// behavior for the modules runtime. So, we currently branch off into an\n\t// entirely-separate codepath in those situations, at the expense of\n\t// duplicating some of the logic for behavior this method would normally\n\t// handle.\n\tif ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed() { // Expand the imports for this resource.\n\t\tknownImports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, true)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\t\tpem := expander.UnknownModuleInstances(n.Addr.Module, false)\n\t\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpandPartial(ctx, moduleInstances, pem, knownImports, unknownImports)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\t\treturn g, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Expand the imports for this resource.\n\timports, unknownImports, importDiags := n.expandResourceImports(ctx, false)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\n\t// Since allowUnknown was set to false in expandResourceImports, we should\n\t// not have any unknown imports.\n\tif unknownImports.Len() > 0 {\n\t\tpanic(\"unexpected unknown imports\")\n\t}\n\n\tg, expandDiags := n.dynamicExpand(ctx, moduleInstances, imports)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(expandDiags)\n\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// Import blocks are expanded in conjunction with their associated resource block.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceImports(ctx EvalContext, allowUnknown bool) (addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// Imports maps the target address to an import ID.\n\tknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]()\n\tunknownImports := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]()\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif len(n.importTargets) == 0 {\n\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Import blocks are only valid within the root module, and must be\n\t// evaluated within that context\n\tctx = evalContextForModuleInstance(ctx, addrs.RootModuleInstance)\n\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\n\tfor _, imp := range n.importTargets {\n\t\tif imp.Config == nil {\n\t\t\t// if we have a legacy addr, it was supplied on the commandline so\n\t\t\t// there is nothing to expand\n\t\t\tif !imp.LegacyAddr.Equal(addrs.AbsResourceInstance{}) {\n\t\t\t\tknownImports.Put(imp.LegacyAddr, cty.StringVal(imp.IDString))\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// legacy import tests may have no configuration\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] no configuration for import target %#v\", imp)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif imp.Config.ForEach == nil {\n\t\t\ttraversal, hds := hcl.AbsTraversalForExpr(imp.Config.To)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(hds)\n\t\t\tto, tds := addrs.ParseAbsResourceInstance(traversal)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tds)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, to, imp.Config.To))\n\n\t\t\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey, allowUnknown)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tknownImports.Put(to, importID)\n\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: found single import target %s\", to)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tforEachData, known, forEachDiags := newForEachEvaluator(imp.Config.ForEach, ctx, allowUnknown).ImportValues()\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(forEachDiags)\n\t\tif forEachDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !known {\n\t\t\t// Then we need to parse the target address as a PartialResource\n\t\t\t// instead of a known resource.\n\t\t\taddr, evalDiags := evalImportUnknownToExpression(imp.Config.To)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// We're going to work out which instances this import block might\n\t\t\t// target actually already exist.\n\t\t\tknownInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n\n\t\t\tcfg := addr.ConfigResource()\n\t\t\tmodInsts := state.ModuleInstances(cfg.Module)\n\t\t\tfor _, modInst := range modInsts {\n\t\t\t\tabs := cfg.Absolute(modInst)\n\t\t\t\tresource := state.Resource(cfg.Absolute(modInst))\n\t\t\t\tif resource == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Then we are creating every instance of this resource.\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tfor inst := range resource.Instances {\n\t\t\t\t\tknownInstances.Add(abs.Instance(inst))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tunknownImports.Put(addr, knownInstances)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, keyData := range forEachData {\n\t\t\tres, evalDiags := evalImportToExpression(imp.Config.To, keyData)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateImportTargetExpansion(n.Config, res, imp.Config.To))\n\n\t\t\timportID, evalDiags := evaluateImportIdExpression(imp.Config.ID, ctx, keyData, allowUnknown)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tknownImports.Put(res, importID)\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] expandResourceImports: expanded import target %s\", res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// filter out any known import which already exist in state\n\tfor _, el := range knownImports.Elements() {\n\t\tif state.ResourceInstance(el.Key) != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] expandResourceImports: skipping import address %s already in state\", el.Key)\n\t\t\tknownImports.Remove(el.Key)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn knownImports, unknownImports, diags\n}\n\n// validateExpandedImportTargets checks that all expanded imports correspond to\n// a configured instance.\n//\n// This function is only called from within the dynamicExpand method, the\n// import validation is inlined within the dynamicExpandPartial method for the\n// alternate code path.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validateExpandedImportTargets(expandedImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], expandedInstances addrs.Set[addrs.Checkable]) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tfor _, addr := range expandedImports.Keys() {\n\t\tif !expandedInstances.Has(addr) {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\", addr),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath == \"\" && len(n.importTargets) == 1 {\n\t\t\t// we have no config and aren't generating any. This isn't caught during\n\t\t\t// validation because generateConfigPath is only a plan option. If we\n\t\t\t// got this far however, it means this node is eligible for config\n\t\t\t// generation, so suggest it to the user.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n\t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n\t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import target %s does not exist. If you wish to automatically generate config for this resource, use the -generate-config-out option within terraform plan. Otherwise, make sure the target resource exists within your configuration. For example:\\n\\n  terraform plan -generate-config-out=generated.tf\", n.Addr),\n\t\t\t\tSubject:  n.importTargets[0].Config.To.Range().Ptr(),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) findOrphans(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance) []*states.Resource {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t// ephemeral resources don't exist in state\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tvar orphans []*states.Resource\n\n\t// Lock the state while we inspect it\n\tsMgr := ctx.State()\n\tstate := sMgr.Lock()\n\n\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, m := range moduleInstances {\n\t\t\tif m.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// The module instance of the resource in the state doesn't exist\n\t\t// in the current config, so this whole resource is orphaned.\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\torphans = append(orphans, res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tsMgr.Unlock()\n\n\treturn orphans\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext, moduleInstances []addrs.ModuleInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar g Graph\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\torphans := n.findOrphans(ctx, moduleInstances)\n\n\tfor _, res := range orphans {\n\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n\t\t\taddr := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\t\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n\t\t\tn := n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs)\n\t\t\tg.Add(n)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// The above dealt with the expansion of the containing module, so now\n\t// we need to deal with the expansion of the resource itself across all\n\t// instances of the module.\n\t//\n\t// We'll gather up all of the leaf instances we learn about along the way\n\t// so that we can inform the checks subsystem of which instances it should\n\t// be expecting check results for, below.\n\n\texpandedInstances := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n\tfor _, module := range moduleInstances {\n\t\tresAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(module)\n\t\tinstances, err := n.expandResourceInstances(ctx, resAddr, imports, &g)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\t\tfor _, instance := range instances {\n\t\t\texpandedInstances.Add(instance)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validateExpandedImportTargets(imports, expandedInstances))\n\n\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\n\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\n\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\n\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\n\t// visiting the checks.\n\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, expandedInstances)\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n\n\treturn &g, diags\n}\n\n// expandResourceInstances calculates the dynamic expansion for the resource\n// itself in the context of a particular module instance.\n//\n// It has several side-effects:\n//   - Adds a node to Graph g for each leaf resource instance it discovers, whether present or orphaned.\n//   - Registers the expansion of the resource in the \"expander\" object embedded inside EvalContext globalCtx.\n//   - Adds each present (non-orphaned) resource instance address to checkableAddrs (guaranteed to always be addrs.AbsResourceInstance, despite being declared as addrs.Checkable).\n//\n// After calling this for each of the module instances the resource appears\n// within, the caller must register the final superset instAddrs with the\n// checks subsystem so that it knows the fully expanded set of checkable\n// object instances for this resource instance.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandResourceInstances(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], g *Graph) ([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// The rest of our work here needs to know which module instance it's\n\t// working in, so that it can evaluate expressions in the appropriate scope.\n\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n\n\t// writeResourceState is responsible for informing the expander of what\n\t// repetition mode this resource has, which allows expander.ExpandResource\n\t// to work below.\n\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Before we expand our resource into potentially many resource instances,\n\t// we'll verify that any mention of this resource in n.forceReplace is\n\t// consistent with the repetition mode of the resource. In other words,\n\t// we're aiming to catch a situation where naming a particular resource\n\t// instance would require an instance key but the given address has none.\n\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n\tinstanceAddrs := expander.ExpandResource(resAddr)\n\n\t// If there's a number of instances other than 1 then we definitely need\n\t// an index.\n\tmustHaveIndex := len(instanceAddrs) != 1\n\t// If there's only one instance then we might still need an index, if the\n\t// instance address has one.\n\tif len(instanceAddrs) == 1 && instanceAddrs[0].Resource.Key != addrs.NoKey {\n\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n\t}\n\tif mustHaveIndex {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n\t}\n\t// NOTE: The actual interpretation of n.forceReplace to produce replace\n\t// actions is in the per-instance function we're about to call, because\n\t// we need to evaluate it on a per-instance basis.\n\n\t// Our graph builder mechanism expects to always be constructing new\n\t// graphs rather than adding to existing ones, so we'll first\n\t// construct a subgraph just for this individual modules's instances and\n\t// then we'll steal all of its nodes and edges to incorporate into our\n\t// main graph which contains all of the resource instances together.\n\tinstG, instDiags := n.resourceInstanceSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, instanceAddrs, imports)\n\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\tg.Subsume(&instG.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n\n\treturn instanceAddrs, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) resourceInstanceSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance, imports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && imports.Len() == 0 {\n\t\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\n\t\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\n\t\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\n\t\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\n\t\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\n\t\treturn &Graph{}, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full state, so we'll\n\t// temporarily lock it while we work on this.\n\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\n\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\n\n\t// Start creating the steps\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t// Expand the count or for_each (if present)\n\t\t&ResourceCountTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResource(ctx, imports, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), n.skipPlanChanges),\n\t\t\tSchema:        n.Schema,\n\t\t\tAddr:          n.ResourceAddr(),\n\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Add the count/for_each orphans\n\t\t&OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete:      n.concreteResourceOrphan,\n\t\t\tAddr:          addr,\n\t\t\tInstanceAddrs: instanceAddrs,\n\t\t\tState:         state,\n\t\t},\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\n\n\t\t// Targeting\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Make sure there is a single root\n\t\t&RootTransformer{},\n\t}\n\n\t// Build the graph\n\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: steps,\n\t\tName:  \"nodeExpandPlannableResource\",\n\t}\n\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\n\n\treturn graph, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResource(ctx EvalContext, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], skipPlanChanges bool) func(*NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\treturn func(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t\tvar m *NodePlannableResourceInstance\n\n\t\t// If we're in legacy import mode (the import CLI command), we only need\n\t\t// to return the import node, not a plannable resource node.\n\t\tfor _, importTarget := range n.importTargets {\n\t\t\tif importTarget.LegacyAddr.Equal(a.Addr) {\n\n\t\t\t\t// If we're in the legacy import mode, then we should never\n\t\t\t\t// see unknown imports. So, it's fine to just look at the known\n\t\t\t\t// imports here.\n\t\t\t\tidValue := knownImports.Get(importTarget.LegacyAddr)\n\n\t\t\t\treturn &graphNodeImportState{\n\t\t\t\t\tAddr:             importTarget.LegacyAddr,\n\t\t\t\t\tID:               idValue.AsString(),\n\t\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\n\t\ta.Config = n.Config\n\t\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n\t\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n\t\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n\t\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n\t\ta.dependsOn = n.dependsOn\n\t\ta.Dependencies = n.dependencies\n\t\ta.preDestroyRefresh = n.preDestroyRefresh\n\t\ta.generateConfigPath = n.generateConfigPath\n\n\t\tm = &NodePlannableResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\n\t\t\t// By the time we're walking, we've figured out whether we need\n\t\t\t// to force on CreateBeforeDestroy due to dependencies on other\n\t\t\t// nodes that have it.\n\t\t\tForceCreateBeforeDestroy: n.CreateBeforeDestroy(),\n\t\t\tskipRefresh:              n.skipRefresh,\n\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:          skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t\tforceReplace:             n.forceReplace,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif importID, ok := knownImports.GetOk(a.Addr); ok {\n\t\t\tm.importTarget = importID\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// We're going to check now if this resource instance *might* be\n\t\t\t// targeted by one of the unknown imports. If it is, we'll set the\n\t\t\t// import target to an unknown value so that the import operation\n\t\t\t// will be deferred.\n\t\t\tfor _, unknownImport := range unknownImports.Elems {\n\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Key.MatchesInstance(a.Addr) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif unknownImport.Value.Has(a.Addr) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// This means that this particular instance already\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// exists within the state. `import` blocks that target\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// instances that already exist are ignored by\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Terraform. This means that even if this unknown\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// import does eventually resolve to this instance then\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// it would be ignored anyway. So for this instance we\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// won't set the import target.\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tm.importTarget = cty.UnknownVal(cty.String)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn m\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) concreteResourceOrphan(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\t// Add the config and state since we don't do that via transforms\n\ta.Config = n.Config\n\ta.ResolvedProvider = n.ResolvedProvider\n\ta.Schema = n.Schema\n\ta.ProvisionerSchemas = n.ProvisionerSchemas\n\ta.ProviderMetas = n.ProviderMetas\n\n\treturn &NodePlannableResourceInstanceOrphan{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: a,\n\t\tskipRefresh:                  n.skipRefresh,\n\t\tskipPlanChanges:              n.skipPlanChanges,\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tfor _, candidateAddr := range n.forceReplace {\n\t\tif candidateAddr.Resource.Key == addrs.NoKey {\n\t\t\tif n.Addr.Resource.Equal(candidateAddr.Resource.Resource) {\n\t\t\t\tswitch {\n\t\t\t\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 0:\n\t\t\t\t\t// In this case there _are_ no instances to replace, so\n\t\t\t\t\t// there isn't any alternative address for us to suggest.\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because this resource doesn't have any instances.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\tcase len(instanceAddrs) == 1:\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\n\\nTo force replacement of the single declared instance, use the following option instead:\\n  -replace=%q\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr, instanceAddrs[0],\n\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t\t\tvar possibleValidOptions strings.Builder\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, addr := range instanceAddrs {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&possibleValidOptions, \"\\n  -replace=%q\", addr)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Warning,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Incompletely-matched force-replace resource instance\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Your force-replace request for %s doesn't match any resource instances because it lacks an instance key.\\n\\nTo force replacement of particular instances, use one or more of the following options instead:%s\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcandidateAddr, possibleValidOptions.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n",

57. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_partial_plan.go",
  "new_string": "NodeResourceInstanceBase",
  "old_string": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_partial_plan.go",
  "newString": "NodeResourceInstanceBase",
  "oldString": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// This file is a temporary split from the node_resource_plan.go file. We handle\n// the unknown instances branch of execution within here, while this is still\n// being developed.\n//\n// We have split the files to make structuring the code easier, eventually once\n// the functions within this file are production ready, we will merge them back\n// into the node_resource_plan.go file.\n\n// dynamicExpandPartial is a variant of dynamicExpand that we use when deferred\n// actions are enabled for the current plan.\n//\n// Once deferred actions are more stable and robust in the stacks runtime, it\n// would be nice to integrate this logic a little better with the main\n// DynamicExpand logic, but it's separate for now to minimize the risk of\n// stacks-specific behavior impacting configurations that are not opted into it.\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) dynamicExpandPartial(ctx EvalContext, knownModules []addrs.ModuleInstance, partialModules addrs.Set[addrs.PartialExpandedModule], knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar g Graph\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tknownResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n\tpartialResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.PartialExpandedResource]()\n\tmaybeOrphanResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n\n\tfor _, moduleAddr := range knownModules {\n\t\tresourceAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(moduleAddr)\n\t\tresources, partials, maybeOrphans, moreDiags := n.expandKnownModule(ctx, resourceAddr, knownImports, unknownImports, &g)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\n\t\t// Track all the resources we know about.\n\t\tknownResources = knownResources.Union(resources)\n\t\tpartialResources = partialResources.Union(partials)\n\t\tmaybeOrphanResources = maybeOrphanResources.Union(maybeOrphans)\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, moduleAddr := range partialModules {\n\t\tresourceAddr := moduleAddr.Resource(n.Addr.Resource)\n\t\tpartialResources.Add(resourceAddr)\n\n\t\t// And add a node to the graph for this resource.\n\t\tg.Add(&nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource{\n\t\t\taddr:             resourceAddr,\n\t\t\tconfig:           n.Config,\n\t\t\tresolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:  n.skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\tfunc() {\n\n\t\tss := ctx.PrevRunState()\n\t\tif ss == nil {\n\t\t\treturn // No previous state, so nothing to do here.\n\t\t}\n\t\tstate := ss.Lock()\n\t\tdefer ss.Unlock()\n\n\tResources:\n\t\tfor _, res := range state.Resources(n.Addr) {\n\n\t\t\tfor _, knownModule := range knownModules {\n\t\t\t\tif knownModule.Equal(res.Addr.Module) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Then we handled this resource as part of the known\n\t\t\t\t\t// modules processing.\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue Resources\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tfor _, partialResource := range partialResources {\n\t\t\t\tif partialResource.MatchesResource(res.Addr) {\n\n\t\t\t\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Then each of the instances is a \"maybe orphan\"\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// instance, and we need to add a node for that.\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmaybeOrphanResources.Add(res.Addr.Instance(key))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(n.concreteResource(ctx, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value](), addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), true)(NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(res.Addr.Instance(key))))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\t// Move onto the next resource.\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue Resources\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Otherwise, everything in here is just a simple orphaned instance.\n\n\t\t\tfor key := range res.Instances {\n\t\t\t\tinst := res.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\t\t\tabs := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(inst)\n\t\t\t\tabs.AttachResourceState(res)\n\t\t\t\tg.Add(n.concreteResourceOrphan(abs))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t}\n\n\t}()\n\n\t// We need to ensure that all of the expanded import targets are actually\n\t// present in the configuration, because we can't import something that\n\t// doesn't exist.\n\t//\n\t// See the validateExpandedImportTargets function for the equivalent of\n\t// this for the known resources path.\nImportValidationKnown:\n\tfor _, addr := range knownImports.Keys() {\n\t\tif knownResources.Has(addr) {\n\t\t\t// Simple case, this is known to be in the configuration so we\n\t\t\t// skip it.\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, partialAddr := range partialResources {\n\t\t\tif partialAddr.MatchesInstance(addr) {\n\t\t\t\t// This is a partial-expanded address, so we can't yet know\n\t\t\t\t// whether it's in the configuration or not, and so we'll\n\t\t\t\t// defer dealing with it to a future round.\n\t\t\t\tcontinue ImportValidationKnown\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif maybeOrphanResources.Has(addr) {\n\t\t\t// This is in the previous state but we can't yet know whether\n\t\t\t// it's still desired, so we'll defer dealing with it to a future\n\t\t\t// round.\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If we get here then the import target is not in the configuration\n\t\t// at all, and so we'll report an error.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\", addr),\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\n\t// We'll also perform the same kind of validation on our unknown imports.\n\t// This will be less precise because we don't have the full state to\n\t// compare against, but we can at least check that the import targets are\n\t// in the configuration.\nImportValidationUnknown:\n\tfor _, elem := range unknownImports.Elems {\n\t\tunknownImport := elem.Key\n\n\t\tfor _, resource := range knownResources {\n\t\t\tif unknownImport.MatchesInstance(resource) {\n\t\t\t\t// This is in the configuration so we can skip it.\n\t\t\t\tcontinue ImportValidationUnknown\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, partialResource := range partialResources {\n\t\t\t// If the partial resource is a subset of the unknown import, or\n\t\t\t// vice versa, then it *might* match up one day once everything\n\t\t\t// is resolved so we'll allow it for now.\n\t\t\tif partialResource.MatchesPartial(unknownImport) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue ImportValidationUnknown\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif unknownImport.MatchesPartial(partialResource) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue ImportValidationUnknown\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, maybeOrphan := range maybeOrphanResources {\n\t\t\tif unknownImport.MatchesInstance(maybeOrphan) {\n\t\t\t\t// This is in the previous state but we can't yet know whether\n\t\t\t\t// it's still desired, so we'll defer dealing with it to a\n\t\t\t\t// future round.\n\t\t\t\tcontinue ImportValidationUnknown\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If we get here then the import target is not in the configuration\n\t\t// at all, and so we'll report an error.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Configuration for import target does not exist\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The configuration for the given import %s does not exist. All target instances must have an associated configuration to be imported.\", unknownImport),\n\t\t))\n\n\t}\n\n\t// If this is a resource that participates in custom condition checks\n\t// (i.e. it has preconditions or postconditions) then the check state\n\t// wants to know the addresses of the checkable objects so that it can\n\t// treat them as unknown status if we encounter an error before actually\n\t// visiting the checks.\n\tif checkState := ctx.Checks(); checkState.ConfigHasChecks(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr) {\n\t\tcheckables := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.Checkable]()\n\t\tfor _, addr := range knownResources {\n\t\t\tcheckables.Add(addr)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor _, addr := range maybeOrphanResources {\n\t\t\tcheckables.Add(addr)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tcheckState.ReportCheckableObjects(n.NodeAbstractResource.Addr, checkables)\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(&g)\n\treturn &g, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) expandKnownModule(globalCtx EvalContext, resAddr addrs.AbsResource, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]], g *Graph) (addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance], addrs.Set[addrs.PartialExpandedResource], addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tmoduleCtx := evalContextForModuleInstance(globalCtx, resAddr.Module)\n\n\tmoreDiags := n.recordResourceData(moduleCtx, resAddr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\texpander := moduleCtx.InstanceExpander()\n\t_, knownInstKeys, haveUnknownKeys := expander.ResourceInstanceKeys(resAddr)\n\n\tknownResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n\tpartialResources := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.PartialExpandedResource]()\n\n\tfor _, key := range knownInstKeys {\n\t\tknownResources.Add(resAddr.Instance(key))\n\t}\n\tif haveUnknownKeys {\n\t\tpartialResources.Add(resAddr.Module.UnexpandedResource(resAddr.Resource))\n\t}\n\n\tmustHaveIndex := len(knownInstKeys) != 1 || haveUnknownKeys\n\tif len(knownInstKeys) == 1 && knownInstKeys[0] != addrs.NoKey {\n\t\tmustHaveIndex = true\n\t}\n\tif mustHaveIndex {\n\t\tvar instanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\t\tfor _, key := range knownInstKeys {\n\t\t\tinstanceAddrs = append(instanceAddrs, resAddr.Instance(key))\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.validForceReplaceTargets(instanceAddrs))\n\t}\n\n\tinstGraph, maybeOrphanResources, instDiags := n.knownModuleSubgraph(moduleCtx, resAddr, knownInstKeys, haveUnknownKeys, knownImports, unknownImports)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(instDiags)\n\tif instDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, nil, diags\n\t}\n\tg.Subsume(&instGraph.AcyclicGraph.Graph)\n\treturn knownResources, partialResources, maybeOrphanResources, diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *nodeExpandPlannableResource) knownModuleSubgraph(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResource, knownInstKeys []addrs.InstanceKey, haveUnknownKeys bool, knownImports addrs.Map[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value], unknownImports addrs.Map[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]]) (*Graph, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance], tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tif n.Config == nil && n.generateConfigPath != \"\" && knownImports.Len() == 0 {\n\t\t// We're generating configuration, but there's nothing to import, which\n\t\t// means the import block must have expanded to zero instances.\n\t\t// the instance expander will always return a single instance because\n\t\t// we have assumed there will eventually be a configuration for this\n\t\t// resource, so return here before we add that to the graph.\n\t\treturn &Graph{}, nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Our graph transformers require access to the full state, so we'll\n\t// temporarily lock it while we work on this.\n\tstate := ctx.State().Lock()\n\tdefer ctx.State().Unlock()\n\n\tmaybeOrphans := addrs.MakeSet[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]()\n\n\tsteps := []GraphTransformer{\n\n\t\tDynamicTransformer(func(graph *Graph) error {\n\t\t\t// We'll add a node for all the known instance keys.\n\t\t\tfor _, key := range knownInstKeys {\n\t\t\t\tgraph.Add(n.concreteResource(ctx, knownImports, unknownImports, n.skipPlanChanges)(NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr.Instance(key))))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}),\n\n\t\tDynamicTransformer(func(graph *Graph) error {\n\t\t\t// We'll add a node if there are unknown instance keys.\n\t\t\tif haveUnknownKeys {\n\t\t\t\tgraph.Add(&nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource{\n\t\t\t\t\taddr:             addr.Module.UnexpandedResource(addr.Resource),\n\t\t\t\t\tconfig:           n.Config,\n\t\t\t\t\tresolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t\t\t\tskipPlanChanges:  n.skipPlanChanges,\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}),\n\n\t\tDynamicTransformer(func(graph *Graph) error {\n\t\t\t// Ephemeral resources don't need to be accounted for in this transform,\n\t\t\t// since they are not in the state.\n\t\t\tif addr.Resource.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// We'll add nodes for any orphaned resources.\n\t\t\trs := state.Resource(addr)\n\t\t\tif rs == nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tInstances:\n\t\t\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n\t\t\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tfor _, knownKey := range knownInstKeys {\n\t\t\t\t\tif knownKey == key {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Then we have a known instance, so we can skip this\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// one - it's definitely not an orphan.\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue Instances\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif haveUnknownKeys {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Then this is a \"maybe orphan\" instance. It isn't mapped\n\t\t\t\t\t// to a known instance but we have unknown keys so we don't\n\t\t\t\t\t// know for sure that it's been deleted.\n\t\t\t\t\tmaybeOrphans.Add(addr.Instance(key))\n\t\t\t\t\tgraph.Add(n.concreteResource(ctx, addrs.MakeMap[addrs.AbsResourceInstance, cty.Value](), addrs.MakeMap[addrs.PartialExpandedResource, addrs.Set[addrs.AbsResourceInstance]](), true)(NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr.Instance(key))))\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// If none of the above, then this is definitely an orphan.\n\t\t\t\tgraph.Add(n.concreteResourceOrphan(NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr.Instance(key))))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}),\n\n\t\t// Attach the state\n\t\t&AttachStateTransformer{State: state},\n\n\t\t// Targeting\n\t\t&TargetsTransformer{Targets: n.Targets},\n\n\t\t// Connect references so ordering is correct\n\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\n\t\t// Make sure there is a single root\n\t\t&RootTransformer{},\n\t}\n\n\tb := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: steps,\n\t\tName:  \"nodeExpandPlannableResource\",\n\t}\n\tgraph, graphDiags := b.Build(addr.Module)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(graphDiags)\n\treturn graph, maybeOrphans, diags\n}\n\n// transformDynamic is a helper struct that wraps a single function, allowing\n// us to transform a graph dynamically.\ntype transformDynamic struct {\n\tTransformer func(*Graph) error\n}\n\n// DynamicTransformer returns a GraphTransformer that will apply the given\n// function to the graph during the dynamic expansion phase.\nfunc DynamicTransformer(f func(*Graph) error) GraphTransformer {\n\treturn &transformDynamic{Transformer: f}\n}\n\n// implements GraphTransformer\nfunc (t *transformDynamic) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\treturn t.Transformer(g)\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": true,
  "structuredPatch": [

        "             // instance, and we need to add a node for that.",

TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer(t *testing.T) {\n\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-basic\")\n\n\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"web\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// The orphan\n\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"db\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// A deposed orphan should not be handled by this transformer\n\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"test_instance\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"deposed\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\tstates.NewDeposedKey(),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"test\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\t})\n\n\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n\t{\n\t\ttf := &ConfigTransformer{Config: mod}\n\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t{\n\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n\t\t\tState:    state,\n\t\t\tConfig:   mod,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceBasicStr)\n\tif actual != expected {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"bad:\\n\\n%s\", actual)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer_countGood(t *testing.T) {\n\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-count\")\n\n\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.IntKey(0)).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule: \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule: {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t{\n\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n\t\t\tState:    state,\n\t\t\tConfig:   mod,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceCountStr)\n\tif actual != expected {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"bad:\\n\\n%s\", actual)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer_countBad(t *testing.T) {\n\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-count-empty\")\n\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: \"foo\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.IntKey(0)).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.IntKey(1)).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule:   addrs.RootModule,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t)\n\t})\n\n\tg := Graph{Path: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n\t{\n\t\ttf := &ConfigTransformer{Config: mod}\n\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t{\n\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n\t\t\tState:    state,\n\t\t\tConfig:   mod,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tactual := strings.TrimSpace(g.String())\n\texpected := strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceCountBadStr)\n\tif actual != expected {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"bad:\\n\\n%s\", actual)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestOrphanResourceInstanceTransformer_modules(t *testing.T) {\n\tmod := testModule(t, \"transform-orphan-modules\")\n\tstate := states.BuildState(func(s *states.SyncState) {\n\t\ts.SetResourceInstanceCurrent(\n\t\t\taddrs.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tMode: addrs.ManagedResourceMode,\n\t\t\t\tType: \"aws_instance\",\n\t\t\t\tName: \"foo\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: addrs.NewDefaultProvider(\"aws\"),\n\t\t\t\tModule: \"web\",\n\t\t\t}.Instance(addrs.NoKey).Absolute(addrs.RootModuleInstance.Child(\"child\", addrs.NoKey)),\n\t\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\t\tAttrsFlat: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": \"foo\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\taddrs.AbsProviderConfig{\n\t\t\t\tProvider: err := tf.Transform(&g); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"err: %s\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t{\n\t\ttf := &OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{\n\t\t\tConcrete: testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc,\n\t\t\tState: strings.TrimSpace(testTransformOrphanResourceModulesStr)\n\tif got != want {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong state result\\ngot:\\n%s\\n\\nwant:\\n%s\", got, want)\n\t}\n}\n\nconst testTransformOrphanResourceBasicStr = `\naws_instance.db (orphan)\naws_instance.web\n`\n\nconst testTransformOrphanResourceCountStr = `\naws_instance.foo\n`\n\nconst testTransformOrphanResourceCountBadStr = `\naws_instance.foo[0] (orphan)\naws_instance.foo[1] (orphan)\n`\n\nconst testTransformOrphanResourceModulesStr = `\naws_instance.foo\nmodule.child.aws_instance.web (orphan)\n`\n\nfunc testOrphanResourceConcreteFunc(a *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) dag.Vertex {\n\treturn &testOrphanResourceInstanceConcrete{a}\n}\n\ntype testOrphanResourceInstanceConcrete struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n}\n\nfunc (n *testOrphanResourceInstanceConcrete) Name() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (orphan)\", n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.Name())\n}\n",

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_import.go",
  "newString": "NodeResourceInstanceBase",
  "oldString": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\ntype graphNodeImportState struct {\n\tAddr             addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addr is the resource address to import into\n\tID               string                    // ID is the ID to import as\n\tProviderAddr     addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // Provider address given by the user, or implied by the resource type\n\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig   // provider node address after resolution\n\n\tstates []providers.ImportedResource\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModulePath        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable        = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer  = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDynamicExpandable = (*graphNodeImportState)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Name() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (import id %q)\", n.Addr, n.ID)\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) ProvidedBy() (addrs.ProviderConfig, bool) {\n\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n\t// name.\n\treturn n.ProviderAddr, false\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Provider() addrs.Provider {\n\t// We assume that n.ProviderAddr has been properly populated here.\n\t// It's the responsibility of the code creating a graphNodeImportState\n\t// to populate this, possibly by calling DefaultProviderConfig() on the\n\t// resource address to infer an implied provider from the resource type\n\t// name.\n\treturn n.ProviderAddr.Provider\n}\n\n// GraphNodeProviderConsumer\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) SetProvider(addr addrs.AbsProviderConfig) {\n\tn.ResolvedProvider = addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeModuleInstance\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module\n}\n\n// GraphNodeModulePath\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) ModulePath() addrs.Module {\n\treturn n.Addr.Module.Module()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// Reset our states\n\tn.states = nil\n\n\tprovider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// import state\n\tabsAddr := n.Addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\n\t\tAddr:         absAddr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n\t}\n\n\t// Call pre-import hook\n\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\treturn h.PreImportState(hookResourceID, n.ID)\n\t}))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tresp := provider.ImportResourceState(providers.ImportResourceStateRequest{\n\t\tTypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,\n\t\tID:       n.ID,\n\t\tClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{\n\t\t\tDeferralAllowed: false,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\timported := resp.ImportedResources\n\tfor _, obj := range imported {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] graphNodeImportState: import %s %q produced instance object of type %s\", absAddr.String(), n.ID, obj.TypeName)\n\t}\n\tn.states = imported\n\n\t// Call post-import hook\n\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\treturn h.PostImportState(hookResourceID, imported)\n\t}))\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// GraphNodeDynamicExpandable impl.\n//\n// We use DynamicExpand as a way to generate the subgraph of refreshes\n// and state inserts we need to do for our import state. Since they're new\n// resources they don't depend on anything else and refreshes are isolated\n// so this is nearly a perfect use case for dynamic expand.\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportState) DynamicExpand(ctx EvalContext) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\tg := &Graph{Path: ctx.Path()}\n\n\t// nameCounter is used to de-dup names in the state.\n\tnameCounter := make(map[string]int)\n\n\t// Compile the list of addresses that we'll be inserting into the state.\n\t// We do this ahead of time so we can verify that we aren't importing\n\t// something that already exists.\n\taddrs := make([]addrs.AbsResourceInstance, len(n.states))\n\tfor i, state := range n.states {\n\t\taddr := n.Addr\n\t\tif t := state.TypeName; t != \"\" {\n\t\t\taddr.Resource.Resource.Type = t\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Determine if we need to suffix the name to de-dup\n\t\tkey := addr.String()\n\t\tcount, ok := nameCounter[key]\n\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\tcount++\n\t\t\taddr.Resource.Resource.Name += fmt.Sprintf(\"-%d\", count)\n\t\t}\n\t\tnameCounter[key] = count\n\n\t\t// Add it to our list\n\t\taddrs[i] = addr\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify that all the addresses are clear\n\tstate := ctx.State()\n\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n\t\texisting := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\tif existing != nil {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Resource already managed by Terraform\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Terraform is already managing a remote object for %s. To import to this address you must first remove the existing object from the state.\", addr),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t// Bail out early, then.\n\t\treturn nil, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// For each of the states, we add a node to handle the refresh/add to state.\n\t// \"n.states\" is populated by our own Execute with the result of\n\t// ImportState. Since DynamicExpand is always called after Execute, this is\n\t// safe.\n\tfor i, state := range n.states {\n\t\tg.Add(&graphNodeImportStateSub{\n\t\t\tTargetAddr:       addrs[i],\n\t\t\tState:            state,\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\taddRootNodeToGraph(g)\n\n\t// Done!\n\treturn g, diags\n}\n\n// graphNodeImportStateSub is the sub-node of graphNodeImportState\n// and is part of the subgraph. This node is responsible for refreshing\n// and adding a resource to the state once it is imported.\ntype graphNodeImportStateSub struct {\n\tTargetAddr       addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n\tState            providers.ImportedResource\n\tResolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance = (*graphNodeImportStateSub)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable     = (*graphNodeImportStateSub)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Name() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"import %s result\", n.TargetAddr)\n}\n\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Path() addrs.ModuleInstance {\n\treturn n.TargetAddr.Module\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable impl.\nfunc (n *graphNodeImportStateSub) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// If the Ephemeral type isn't set, then it is an error\n\tif n.State.TypeName == \"\" {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"import of %s didn't set type\", n.TargetAddr.String()))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate := n.State.AsInstanceObject()\n\n\t// Refresh\n\triNode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\tAddr: n.TargetAddr,\n\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\tResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tstate, deferred, refreshDiags := riNode.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, state, false)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// If the refresh is deferred we will need to do another cycle to import the resource\n\tif deferred != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Cannot import deferred remote object\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"the provider deferred reading the resource. \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"This is a bug in the provider since deferrals are not supported when importing through the CLI, please file an issue.\"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Please either use an import block for importing this resource \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"or remove the to be imported resource from your configuration, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"apply the configuration using \\\"terraform apply\\\", \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\"add the to be imported resource again, and retry the import operation.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.TargetAddr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Verify the existance of the imported resource\n\t\tif state.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Cannot import non-existent remote object\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"While attempting to import an existing object to %q, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"the provider detected that no object exists with the given id. \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Only pre-existing objects can be imported; check that the id \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"is correct and that it is associated with the provider's \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"configured region or endpoint, or use \\\"terraform apply\\\" to \"+\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"create a new remote object for this resource.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tn.TargetAddr,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(riNode.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\n\treturn diags\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": true,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "   state := n.State.AsInstanceObject()",
        " ",
        "   // Refresh",
        "-  riNode := &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{",

        "     NodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{",
        "       ResolvedProvider: n.ResolvedProvider,"

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_forget.go",
  "newString": "NodeResourceInstanceBase",
  "oldString": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\n// NodeForgetResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be\n// removed from state.\ntype NodeForgetResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource      = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable          = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProviderConsumer    = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeProvisionerConsumer = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDestroyer           = (*NodeForgetResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) DestroyAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\treturn &n.Addr\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Name() string {\n\treturn n.ResourceInstanceAddr().String() + \" (forget)\"\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool) {\n\tif n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode == addrs.DataResourceMode {\n\t\t// Indicate that this node does not require a configured provider\n\t\treturn nil, true\n\t}\n\treturn n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable\nfunc (n *NodeForgetResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tis := n.instanceState\n\tif is == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] NodeForgetResourceInstance for %s with no state\", addr)\n\t}\n\n\tvar changeApply *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tchangeApply, err = n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif changeApply == nil || diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Exit early if state is already null\n\tif state == nil || state.Value.IsNull() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tctx.State().ForgetResourceInstanceCurrent(n.Addr)\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\treturn diags\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": true,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " // NodeForgetResourceInstance represents a resource instance that is to be",
        " // removed from state.",
        " type NodeForgetResourceInstance struct {",
        "-  *NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
        "+  *NodeResourceInstanceBase",
        " }",
        " ",
        " var ("
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 16,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 16
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "     // Indicate that this node does not require a configured provider",
        "     return nil, true",
        "   }",
        "-  return n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.ProvidedBy()",
        "+  return n.NodeResourceInstanceBase.ProvidedBy()",

        " // GraphNodeExecutable"
      ],

65. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_orphan_count.go",
  "new_string": "NodeResourceInstanceBase",
  "old_string": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
  "replace_all": true
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_orphan_count.go",
  "newString": "NodeResourceInstanceBase",
  "oldString": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\n// OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds orphans\n// for an expanded count to the graph. The determination of this depends\n// on the count argument given.\n//\n// Orphans are found by comparing the count to what is found in the state.\n// This transform assumes that if an element in the state is within the count\n// bounds given, that it is not an orphan.\ntype OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer struct {\n\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\n\tAddr          addrs.AbsResource           // Addr of the resource to look for orphans\n\tInstanceAddrs []addrs.AbsResourceInstance // Addresses that currently exist in config\n\tState         *states.State               // Full global state\n}\n\nfunc (t *OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\trs := t.State.Resource(t.Addr)\n\tif rs == nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Resource doesn't exist in state, so nothing to do!\n\t}\n\n\t// This is an O(n*m) analysis, which we accept for now because the\n\t// number of instances of a single resource ought to always be small in any\n\t// reasonable Terraform configuration.\nHave:\n\tfor key, inst := range rs.Instances {\n\t\t// Instances which have no current objects (only one or more\n\t\t// deposed objects) will be taken care of separately\n\t\tif inst.Current == nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tthisAddr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)\n\t\tfor _, wantAddr := range t.InstanceAddrs {\n\t\t\tif wantAddr.Equal(thisAddr) {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue Have\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If thisAddr is not in t.InstanceAddrs then we've found an \"orphan\"\n\n\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(thisAddr)\n\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] OrphanResourceInstanceCountTransformer: adding %s as %T\", thisAddr, node)\n\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": true,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     }",
        "     // If thisAddr is not in t.InstanceAddrs then we've found an \"orphan\"",
        " ",
        "-    abstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(thisAddr)",
        "+    abstract := NewNodeResourceInstanceBase(thisAddr)",
        "     var node dag.Vertex = abstract",
        "     if f := t.Concrete; f != nil {",
        "       node = f(abstract)"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 51,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 51
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

  "filePath": "/workspace/internal/terraform/node_resource_destroy_deposed_test.go",

  "oldString": "NodeAbstractResourceInstance",
  "originalFile": "// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/deferring\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n)\n\nfunc TestNodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject_Execute(t *testing.T) {\n\tdeposedKey := states.NewDeposedKey()\n\tstate := states.NewState()\n\tabsResource := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_instance.foo\")\n\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n\t\tabsResource.Resource,\n\t\tdeposedKey,\n\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectTainted,\n\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"bar\"}`),\n\t\t},\n\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n\t)\n\n\tp := testProvider(\"test\")\n\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n\tp.UpgradeResourceStateResponse = &providers.UpgradeResourceStateResponse{\n\t\tUpgradedState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"bar\"),\n\t\t}),\n\t}\n\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n\t\tStateState:        state.SyncWrapper(),\n\t\tPrevRunStateState: state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n\t\tRefreshStateState: state.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper(),\n\t\tProviderProvider:  p,\n\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: providers.ProviderSchema{\n\t\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n\t\t\t\t\"test_instance\": {\n\t\t\t\t\tBlock: &configschema.Block{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tComputed: true,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tChangesChanges: plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n\t}\n\n\tnode := NodePlanDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tAddr: absResource,\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tDeposedKey: deposedKey,\n\t}\n\terr := node.Execute(ctx, walkPlan)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !p.UpgradeResourceStateCalled {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"UpgradeResourceState wasn't called; should've been called to upgrade the previous run's object\")\n\t}\n\tif !p.ReadResourceCalled {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"ReadResource wasn't called; should've been called to refresh the deposed object\")\n\t}\n\n\tchange := ctx.Changes().GetResourceInstanceChange(absResource, deposedKey)\n\tif got, want := change.Change.Action, plans.Delete; got != want {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"wrong planned action\\ngot:  %s\\nwant: %s\", got, want)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestNodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject_Execute(t *testing.T) {\n\tdeposedKey := states.NewDeposedKey()\n\tstate := states.NewState()\n\tabsResource := mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_instance.foo\")\n\tstate.Module(addrs.RootModuleInstance).SetResourceInstanceDeposed(\n\t\tabsResource.Resource,\n\t\tdeposedKey,\n\t\t&states.ResourceInstanceObjectSrc{\n\t\t\tStatus:    states.ObjectTainted,\n\t\t\tAttrsJSON: []byte(`{\"id\":\"bar\"}`),\n\t\t},\n\t\tmustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n\t)\n\n\tschema := providers.ProviderSchema{\n\t\tResourceTypes: map[string]providers.Schema{\n\t\t\t\"test_instance\": {\n\t\t\t\tBlock: &configschema.Block{\n\t\t\t\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"id\": {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tComputed: true,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tp := testProvider(\"test\")\n\tp.ConfigureProvider(providers.ConfigureProviderRequest{})\n\tp.GetProviderSchemaResponse = &schema\n\n\tp.UpgradeResourceStateResponse = &providers.UpgradeResourceStateResponse{\n\t\tUpgradedState: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"bar\"),\n\t\t}),\n\t}\n\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n\t\tStateState:           state.SyncWrapper(),\n\t\tProviderProvider:     p,\n\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: schema,\n\t\tChangesChanges:       plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n\t\tDeferralsState:       deferring.NewDeferred(false),\n\t}\n\n\tnode := NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tAddr: absResource,\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tDeposedKey: deposedKey,\n\t}\n\terr := node.Execute(ctx, walkApply)\n\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !state.Empty() {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"resources left in state after destroy\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestNodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject_WriteResourceInstanceState(t *testing.T) {\n\tstate := states.NewState()\n\tctx := new(MockEvalContext)\n\tctx.StateState = state.SyncWrapper()\n\tctx.Scope = evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: addrs.RootModuleInstance}\n\tmockProvider := mockProviderWithResourceTypeSchema(\"aws_instance\", &configschema.Block{\n\t\tAttributes: map[string]*configschema.Attribute{\n\t\t\t\"id\": {\n\t\t\t\tType:     cty.String,\n\t\t\t\tOptional: true,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tctx.ProviderProvider = mockProvider\n\tctx.ProviderSchemaSchema = mockProvider.GetProviderSchema()\n\n\tobj := &states.ResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\tValue: cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\n\t\t\t\"id\": cty.StringVal(\"i-abc123\"),\n\t\t}),\n\t\tStatus: states.ObjectReady,\n\t}\n\tnode := &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]`),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"aws_instance.foo\"),\n\t\t},\n\t\tDeposedKey: states.NewDeposedKey(),\n\t}\n\terr := node.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, obj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"unexpected error: %s\", err.Error())\n\t}\n\n\tcheckStateString(t, state, `\naws_instance.foo: (1 deposed)\n  ID = <not created>\n  provider = provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/aws\"]\n  Deposed ID 1 = i-abc123\n\t`)\n}\n\nfunc TestNodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject_ExecuteMissingState(t *testing.T) {\n\tp := simpleMockProvider()\n\tctx := &MockEvalContext{\n\t\tStateState:           states.NewState().SyncWrapper(),\n\t\tProviderProvider:     simpleMockProvider(),\n\t\tProviderSchemaSchema: p.GetProviderSchema(),\n\t\tChangesChanges:       plans.NewChanges().SyncWrapper(),\n\t}\n\n\tnode := NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: &NodeAbstractResourceInstance{\n\t\t\tAddr: mustResourceInstanceAddr(\"test_object.foo\"),\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResource: NodeAbstractResource{\n\t\t\t\tResolvedProvider: mustProviderConfig(`provider[\"registry.terraform.io/hashicorp/test\"]`),\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tDeposedKey: states.NewDeposedKey(),\n\t}\n\terr := node.Execute(ctx, walkApply)\n\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"expected error\")\n\t}\n}\n",
